transcript.text: All set.
Great.
How's it going?
Perfect.
Perfect.
Going well.
It's going well.
How have been?
Terrific.
Yeah, very good.
Thanks.
Appreciate you taking some time.

So, yeah, we had some time to talk internally, and I wanted to go over the process that you outlined earlier and just kind of give you some more context on, like, what we're prioritizing, and, yeah, and then love to get your thoughts on next steps.
Yeah, that sounds great.
Okay.

So, just going off of memory, if I recall correctly, there's a 12-month commitment, but prior to that, then you have a program where we can do a, I can't recall the exact weeks, but X weeks of sort of getting up to speed and seeing if it is indeed a good fit for between the two companies.
And if so, then we launch into that 12-month agreement.
So, first off, is that my understanding correct?
Absolutely.
So, we have two phases.
Phase one is what we...
We call a strategy sprint.
It's eight weeks.
You have until the last week of that eight-week period to opt out from phase two, which is the 12-month period.

And I guess one more piece of information around phase one, we are really using that to focus on showing you the quality we can hit with the content that we write and the roadmap that we would execute on over the next six months.

It's not enough time to see super strong performance signals, but we basically use the eight weeks to develop the context engineering for our workflows to be hyper-personalized to nooks and effective.
And so we're doing that and a combination of creating calibration articles so that you've got 10-plus articles published by that opt-out period.
Okay.
And just introducing Natasha, who runs her lifecycle marketing and is...
She's been helping also with our SEO, GEO work.
Nice to meet you, Tyler.
Yeah, great to meet you, Natasha.
Congrats on the role at Nooks.
I see you've been there for about five months.
Thank you.
Yeah.
So in that phase one, is it, obviously we want to get tuned.
We want to make sure that the content being produced is aligned.
But are there actual deliverables in that span?
Like, are we actually going be producing content?
Because what I don't want to do is be sharpening the axe for eight weeks.
A hundred percent.
In week one or two, we'll send the artifacts.
So that's going to come really early.
We're going to give you guys, like, the editorial roadmap, probably week three.
And then we would start creating the calibration content around the same time.
And then publish about 10 articles by that opt-out in week seven, week eight.
So you'd have about 10 articles from us in a day.
to all the deliverables that you see here.
Got it.
And let's see.
then one of the things that was appealing to me when you were presenting earlier was it wasn't only like a blog post, but it was more sophisticated type content.
Is that something that would be in phase one or would that only be pending phase two?
That would probably be more phase two, and we'd really look at it based on the opportunity, right?

Like when we dug into Nooks's, you know, SEMrush dashboard and the pages that have been indexed, when we looked at, you know, the competitor landscape, I mean, one thing that you brought up, right, is there's a huge opportunity with non-branded.
And then there's also just an opportunity to create, at a much higher scale, some of the blog posts that...
That you've created to date, right?
So that would be like the first place we would start to generate results the fastest.
And then we could move into more like thought leadership type of content, maybe even a programmatic play.
But, you know, I think like the biggest need for you guys right now is just really being able to scale a lot of the editorial content that you've started to create that is developing some traction.
And then like we can obviously sort of fine tune the engine after we've got a good foundation.
So it would be pages similar to like what's performing well currently from the blog as the starting point.
Okay, so one of the things that we're considering is there's a number of GEO analytics tools that also do like recommendations on which content to produce.
Um, and, um, and...

I'm sure your analysis is similarly or perhaps better, but I want to, I guess when I think about the complexity of going from, okay, this is the content outline that you should produce that these other tools would generate for us.
They don't generate the content, but they do recommend it.
I'm just trying to understand what would be the value, quantifying the value that you would add in addition to what these other tools that we're looking at are adding.
That's not very well structured.
Let me restructure that again.
We're looking at tools that do the analysis and then recommendations.
And so what I understand from, from the growthx value, it's analysis, recommendations, and then execution on those recommendations.
And so with the other options, they are not doing the execution, but, you know, how, you know, is, is that.
Is that value for the actual execution worth the significantly higher spend?
I hear what you're saying.
Yeah, I think, you know, you'd have to think of us more as an agency versus like a software platform.
And what we've seen is that the recommendations, you know, those are all going to come from like keyword analysis, search volume, competitor content.

But unless we're actually digging in as deep as possible into your ICP, the topics where you want to have authority, creating content with much more sophisticated workflows than, right, like what one of these products could give.
You might fall flat with like the content you create being the best answer to your ICP's questions.
You might fall flat with how quickly you're generating citations, right, driving traffic.
And so just to give you like a quick example here, when you think about...
So the content that we've done for outreach, right?
Which this seems simple, but it's really focused on like, where's the SEO opportunity?
Eight warning signs, your deals at risk, how to fix them, cross-linking and all the content, sales capacity planning models, build smarter revenue teams.
But then also look at exec, right?
30 role play scenarios you can use to sharpen your skills.
This is like a much more in-depth piece of content that provides all these different scenarios that are specific to the sales training, like that exec provides as a business.
This next one, you know, most common sales objections, more informational, but like, you need a piece of content around that, right?
Like, and you need to create that at scale if you want to drive results.
If you look at the artifacts that we created for exec, the personas artifacts, we're getting super specific around the nuance, right?
So like the results driven sales enable.
Who are they?
What's their daily reality?
How do you win their trust?
How do you lose their trust?
The strategic learning and development director, et cetera, et cetera.
The revenue focused chief revenue officer.
No brainer that the chief revenue officer is focused on revenue.
Unless you do all this context engineering, right?

And combine that with workflows that are sophisticated enough to take that context, use different models, perform agentic research, generate drafts that the experts have the ability to be in the loop to make changes towards, doing the fact checking.
There's all these other pieces that come with creating a great piece of content.
And just having this idea of what to create doesn't solve any of like the execution itself.
And to be able to say, you know, hey, growthx, be an extension of our content team, right?
It's like me and my product marketing leader.
We're creating content today.

What if you were able to create like the highest caliber content, publish every single day, have all of the last mile execution staging and publishing taken care of, be given the analytics around like where are you showing up, where is the data pointing us in terms of where to go next.
We're basically taking all of that off your team's plate, whereas one of these platforms can do a good job of showcasing like the AI visibility and monitoring, maybe generate some ideas.
But is that like being done by a system or is that being done by SEO and content marketing experts?
That's one differentiator around identifying the right opportunities.

But then the other piece is, well, how can you create the best version of that content when there are competitors that, right, are going to maybe have teams manually write it or, you know, maybe they're using AI to create at a much larger quantity than Nooks is just based on the amount of index pages you have to date.
But a lot.
There, you know, definitely more we can unpack, but hopefully that sheds some light.
Right.
Okay.
Got it.
Thank you.
What other questions do you have?
I definitely want to make this as relevant as I can for you.
Yeah.
So back to Phase 1, we're just always optimizing for speed.
So, like, when can we start getting impact?
When can we start publishing in Phase 1 is sort of the biggest question.
Like, you know, we've just, we've got big goals.
We've got to get moving if we're going to do this approach.
It makes a lot of sense.
And I guess, you know, does, like, your team feel like the gap that needs to be filled in is being able to create, like, quality content at a much higher scale?
Is editorial what you feel like you need a partner to help you execute on?
Yeah.
Yeah.
I think that is a major thing, but it's going beyond blog posts.
Like blog posts, can, you know, use, you know, if it's, you know, getting the key pillar blog posts up, I think we're okay on.

We definitely don't have the pace and volume, and we definitely don't have the space for doing non-blog, you know, content, maybe like, you know, more thought leadership type material or more experience type content, you know, where you're, you know, maybe it's an ROI calculator or some such.
That makes sense.

And, um, is there a need for like comparison content, you know, um, you know, definitions, um, when you talk about thought leadership, do you have an example that our team could look at to make sure there's a, there's a good fit?
Yeah, I, I mean, that one I kind of worry about.
Because like, if it's comparison with competitors, we definitely want to own that internally.
If it's like, I don't know, I guess maybe that's sort of the concern I guess I have is like, how much is too much?
Like, is it really necessary to publish as frequently as we would in this program?
Like, does that actually have net benefit or is the first three months of production, you know, really get us over the hump?
And then it's a matter of, you know, coming back and, you know, just occasionally adding additional content versus like a blistering pace of content.
We definitely feel like quantity is important if you can have quality at the scale that you need.
That's what we're really good at.
When you think about AI search, you have to embrace the long tail.
You have to think in terms of...

With the ICP that we're going after, what are all the questions that we can help them answer that may not be directly connected to our product, but may help someone get to our website and explore our product that is in like a sales team or a RevOps team.
And so, you know, that's why like a lot of the content that we're creating for outreach, you know, it's content around capacity planning, deals, with exec, right?
It's everything connected to what they could train around.
With Ramp, it's anything connected to business spending.
So I think we definitely embrace quantity.

If you were looking for growthx to create, you know, a report that's 20 pages that you publish once a year or once a quarter, that wouldn't be a great fit for what we typically bring on clients to do.
I think over time, like we can get more.
Custom.
There's no doubt about it.
We could think about these calculators that you're talking about and these tools, but how the partnership would start would be us going in and creating and refreshing content on a business day basis.

And we believe that that will have the fastest ROI for you if we can really do it in a way that's audience first and connected to competitor content where there's gaps, topics where it makes sense for you to have authority, and some of that's more nuanced.
You know, engine in the business travel space.

Look at all of these different topics that we create content around, travel booking and reservations, travel management and corporate travel, travel disruptions, but we also do industry specific content around travel.
We do content around destinations.
And so, you know, a lot of what we think about when it comes to scale is...

There are so many different places where you could create content that's connected to some of those fears, some of those motivations, some of those things that your ICP might be thinking about but doesn't have a direct connection to your product, right, but plants the seed to get them on your website or has a direct connection, right, parallel dialing.
Like, we create as much content as we can around that space.
It's all rooted in, like, what's the search volume?
Is there actual opportunity?
Can you capture it from, like, Orem and Reggie and even other competitors that are maybe larger sales software platforms?
So, you know, I say all this just to make sure that, like, if we do move forward with this partnership, it's connected to something that you also feel like is going to drive value.
But, you know, we're going to measure our performance based on your traffic and based on the amount of traffic that we're helping you convert.
All right, Natasha, any questions?
Not particularly.
I'm still trying to, I think, process, like, how just the benefit of how this will help us with, like, AEO a little bit more.
So, like, I have a question.
I do have a question.
So on your head, so are you able to see our, like, rankings and how we perform in, like, AI search?
And then from there, that's, like, part of the bundle to be, like, okay, this is where you're ranking, visibility.
And then you give, like, content recommendations.
And then I guess just a caveat to that, like, something else that, like, we need to think about as well is the right prompts.
What are the prompts that are more important for us when it comes to AEO?
And then, yeah, and then from there, the recommendation based on the searches that are more important to us, where we think we're going to win more.
Absolutely.
Absolutely, Natasha.
I would say, like, that's a huge focus in these engagements, right?
Like, augment code is an example we use often, just because we have created content at such a large scale for them.
Every single guide that you see in this slash guides section is a piece of content that we created.
And the searches they want to show up for are, like, AI enterprise coding.
And, like, one differentiator they have is they can handle large code bases better than Cursor and even better than GitHub in some circumstances.
And so with augment code, you know, we set up this dashboard and we have our own internal platform that we're moving this to over the next three to six months.
But with this, you can see that it's tracking all the different platforms.
It's tracking their visibility against competitors.
It's tracking sentiment, position, in terms of where they show up in the answers.
And then there's, like, 2,500 individual prompts where we're tracking their visibility daily, right?
We're showing them, like, which citation.
And we actually have, you know, all these workflows that we use to create content, I showed Peter some of this last time we chatted.
But you know, these workflows are used for everything from figuring out what those prompts are, that we should track based on looking at third party platforms, LinkedIn, Reddit forums.
But they're, you they're also used to create the brief for the assignment to do the deep research to generate the draft.
And so, you know, in this example right here, we have provided these artifacts as the context, a topic that we want to write about.
And then the output is this brief, but what's happening in the background is we're doing all of these steps and processing all of these tokens so that we can look at the keyword traffic.
The organic results, the SERP ranking factors, the competitor content.
And so what happens on our side is, you know, we use these workflows.
To scale quality, and it's only as effective as the artifacts, the context that we create, and the conversations that we have with your team.

And so like we have conversations with your team, we ingest resources, we do a lot so that, you know, our biggest benefit is you have a ton of time freed up and we're basically like creating this content on your behalf.
But if you look into like our workflows library, know, one of the, one of the, there we go, topic clustering workflow.

So one of the different workflows that we have here is going to go in and basically generate like all of these prompts that we should track connected to real conversations that are happening in other tools.
Um, I haven't really shown this one, of course, is, is probably the best one for me to start with, but, you know, if you take like our researcher.
With our researcher.

We're going in and we're creating a plan, we're generating these research questions, we're evaluating the quality in a loop, and we're storing our research to optimize for the next piece of content that we create.

And then the actual activities, right, there's all of these different models that we're using, so we'll use Claude, we'll use Exa, and Tavly, and Perplexity, and OpenAI, and then we've got these very complicated prompts that are combined with the context for every single piece of content, for every step that we perform as part of the content creation process.

I answered more than you asked there, but, like, just trying to give you a look into, like, how sophisticated some of the workflows that we've built are and how this process is, like, one that we customize for each client, but follow, like, the key milestones, which is generating the artifacts, creating the roadmap of assignments based on the SEO opportunity, pulling in prompts that we should track based on what action

Actually lives in these different platforms and is connected to the topics where you want authority and then going in and creating that content at scale with experts in the loop for each step of the process.
Also, no, this is really cool.
like seeing the back end just to understand how everything comes together.
So this is great.
Thanks for walking through it.
Yeah, no problem.
I guess, Peter, when you think about like growthx and other options, you know, I guess what other options are you sort of thinking about when it comes to being able to execute here?

Yeah, think that's fundamentally the, what I was saying earlier is like we have access to GEO tools that do analytics, of see, know, track over time, what our share of voice and, you know, how often we come up in queries.
They make some recommendations on terms of what kind of content to produce, but they don't do the.
Execution.
So it's a matter of weighting that execution side of things.
Yeah, that's it.
I guess, is there anything else that I can help provide clarity around or answer to help you think through how important the execution piece is or where we differentiate there?
Yeah.
mean, any, any other things that you can do like for that around that, that would be helpful.

You know, yeah, because I, the thing that I worry about is just if we, if we use a, you know, clod or whatever for taking the prompt and then creating content, are we going to get 90% of the way there on our own?
You know, versus, you know, it may don't be as good, but it, but is it?
Is it as close considering, you know, it's not a small investment, right?
Yeah, that makes sense.
You know, I think most clients we work with, have a strong opinion that this is like the place where they want to invest at scale.
A lot of times our strategy sprint can show you the quality that we can hit and then the assignments that we can go out and execute on.

But I would say, you know, ultimately, you know, like you could build this internally and a lot of customers see benefit in terms of ROI and even costs over the long term of having our team, you know, create it and process all these tokens, leverage all these different tools.

And you can also think in terms of, you know, other use cases for generating pipe, other options for generating pipeline, whether it's using Nooks and having like a heavy outbound motion, whether it's building more...

Community, you're having more dated assets, but what our bread and butter is would be directly improving your AI visibility and creating traffic that is higher intent because it's content that's created audience first, content that's created with that insight into not how do we create content for sales leaders, but how do we create content for the type of sales leaders that are great customers, you know, for your team?
And, you know, I think it's this approach where we're using AI to speed up the process as much as possible, but only after we have a really good perspective on why is Nooks different in the market?
How is Nooks a better solution than Orem or Reggie or whatever it might be or, you know, doing nothing?
And then, you know, we would create all the content, refresh all the content, and then move to other use cases when it makes sense.
Yes, but it would be.
We like editorial as the starting point.
And, you know, I think you can see where you can get with some of these tools.
And sometimes that is the best place to start, right?
If you're not completely confident in this white glove service.
But, you know, we are really just trying to, like, be an extension of your team so that you don't have to worry about creating content.
You can focus on strategic initiatives or even the pillar pieces that you would create quarterly or yearly.
Right.
Okay.
Got it.
Okay, Tyler, we'll circle back internally and then come back to you.
But feel free to ping me.
know, generally, we do try to move quickly.
So hopefully we can talk soon.
Yeah, definitely.
And I know that, you know, Marcel can give you, like, a much deeper level of insight than I can a couple months in here.
So if you do feel like you need that conversation with Marcel to make the right decision, don't hesitate to ask.
And I know he'll want to join the call.
Okay.
That's great.
Thanks,
Thank you.
Great to see you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review GrowthX's partnership model and Nooks's key priorities.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=8.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Partnership Model:** GrowthX's model starts with an 8-week "Strategy Sprint" to prove quality with \~10 articles before a 12-month commitment.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=30.0)
  - [**Value Proposition:** GrowthX is an agency providing full execution, contrasting with Nooks's current SEO tools that only provide recommendations.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=324.0)
  - [**Nooks's Priorities:** Nooks needs rapid, high-volume content, but also wants more complex formats (e.g., calculators) and questions the ROI of a "blistering pace."](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=619.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Approach:** The strategy is to first scale high-volume editorial content for fast ROI, then introduce complex formats.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=188.0)

## Topics

### Nooks's Priorities & Concerns

  - [**Primary Need:** Rapid, high-volume content production.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=619.0)
  - [**Desired Content Mix:**](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [High-volume editorial (blog posts).](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=230.0)
      - [More complex formats (e.g., ROI calculators, thought leadership).](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [**Core Concern:** Justifying the investment over using internal tools and AI (e.g., Claude) to get "90% of the way there."](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=1352.0)
  - [**Pace Question:** Is a "blistering pace" of content production necessary, or would an initial burst suffice?](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)

### GrowthX's Partnership Model

  - [**Phase 1: 8-Week Strategy Sprint**](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=56.0)
      - [**Purpose:** Prove quality and build a 6-month roadmap.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=70.0)
      - [**Opt-Out:** Nooks can cancel before the 12-month agreement.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=52.0)
      - [**Deliverables:**](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=134.0)
          - [Editorial roadmap (Week 3).](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=142.0)
          - [\~10 "calibration" articles published by Week 7/8.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
  - [**Phase 2: 12-Month Agreement**](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=30.0)
      - [**Focus:** Scale editorial content for fast ROI.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=841.0)
      - [**Future Work:** Introduce complex formats (calculators) based on opportunity.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=188.0)

### GrowthX's Value Proposition & Process

  - [**Agency vs. Tool:** GrowthX is an agency providing full execution, not just recommendations.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=369.0)
  - [**Execution Workflow:**](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
      - [**Context Engineering:** Deeply understand Nooks's ICP and market differentiators.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
      - [**Sophisticated AI Workflows:** Use multiple models (Claude, OpenAI) for agentic research, drafting, and fact-checking.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=1212.0)
      - [**Expert Oversight:** Human experts are in the loop at each step.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=502.0)
  - [**AEO (AI Engine Optimization):**](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=964.0)
      - [Tracks visibility against competitors across \~2,500 prompts.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=1050.0)
      - [Uses real-world conversations (Reddit, LinkedIn) to find high-value prompts.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
      - [**Example:** For Augment Code, tracking visibility for "AI enterprise coding" and its unique ability to handle large codebases.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=1021.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Nooks:** Discuss internally to weigh the value of GrowthX's full execution service against using internal tools.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=1534.0)
  - [**GrowthX:** Offer a follow-up call with Marcel for a deeper technical discussion if needed.](https://fathom.video/share/zFbNSjvg-_TCgnvzjH9wtfT4Ab5LrVjm?tab=summary&timestamp=1544.0)

action_items.description: Align internally w/ Natasha on GrowthX proposal; decide go/no-go; update Tyler
